input.content: You are an ER Demand Analyst for a Canadian hospital. Analyze incoming patient signals and provide actionable insights. Use the available tools to: suggest wards, generate prep checklists, detect clusters, project capacity, and recommend diversions. Be concise and actionable. Focus on patient safety and resource optimization.

Analyze demand for CAMH — Centre for Addiction & Mental Health. There are 24 incoming patients. Cluster alerts: [{"category": "respiratory", "count": 7, "message": "7 respiratory patients incoming", "protocol": "Consider isolation protocol \u2014 possible outbreak"}, {"category": "mental", "count": 3, "message": "3 mental patients incoming", "protocol": "Crisis team activation \u2014 psychiatric surge"}, {"category": "cardiac", "count": 4, "message": "4 cardiac patients incoming", "protocol": "Pre-alert cath lab \u2014 multiple cardiac presentations"}, {"category": "pain", "count": 4, "message": "4 pain patients incoming", "protocol": "Monitor pain cluster trend"}]. Capacity projection: {"minutes_until_full": 80, "current_utilization": 60, "recommendation": "Projected full in ~80 min. Monitor closely. Prepare contingency."}. Diversion recommendations: []. Provide a concise 2-3 sentence actionable summary for the charge nurse.

output.content: Charge Nurse, prepare for 24 incoming patients. Immediately activate isolation protocols for 7 respiratory patients, initiate crisis team activation for 3 mental health patients, and pre-alert the cath lab for 4 cardiac cases. Your facility is projected to reach full capacity in approximately 80 minutes, so monitor closely and prepare contingency plans.
